text.title: PMBoK Performance Domains Overview Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding PMBoK Performance Domains Overview. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Assessment MUST score all eight PMBoK 7 domains (Stakeholder, Team, Development Approach, Planning, Project Work, Delivery, Measurement, Uncertainty). Skipping a domain is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Skipping domains creates blind spots; the rubric's value is portfolio-comparable coverage.

rule.statement: Each domain score (red/amber/green) MUST cite ≥1 evidence row (artefact reference, observation date, or metric).
rule.rationale: Unevidenced RAG scores are vibes; evidence forces honest assessment.

rule.statement: Every amber or red score MUST carry a recommended action with owner + due date.
rule.rationale: Scores without actions are diagnostic without therapeutic; the rule closes the loop.

rule.statement: Performance-domain rubric MUST be re-run at minimum quarterly. Annual-only cadence is forbidden for projects >6 months.
rule.rationale: Annual cadence lets domains drift unnoticed; quarterly catches early signal.

rule.statement: Scorer MUST be independent of the project (PMO, peer PM, external auditor). Self-scoring forbidden.
rule.rationale: Self-scoring inflates greens; independent scoring exposes signals the PM minimises.

rule.statement: If preconditions for PMBoK Performance Domains Overview fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying PMBoK Performance Domains Overview outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
